example: Jet lag does that to everyone honestly.
zh: 時差反應，身體還在原本時區的時間狀態。
ja: 時差ぼけ、体がまだ元の時間帯のままである状態。

example: Hotels call this a continental breakfast.
zh: 歐式早餐，通常包含麵包、水果和冷肉。
ja: コンチネンタルブレックファースト、通常パン、フルーツ、冷たい肉が含まれる朝食。

example: That is a full English breakfast.
zh: 英式全套早餐，包括蛋、豆子、香腸、吐司、培根等。
ja: フルイングリッシュブレックファースト、卵、豆、ソーセージ、トースト、ベーコンなどを含む朝食。

example: Of course, it is complimentary, help yourself.
ja: 無料の、部屋代に含まれている。

example: You need to report that to reception.
ja: 報告する、問題を伝えること。

example: That means they will send maintenance up.
zh: 維修人員，負責修理的人。
ja: メンテナンス担当者、修理を行う人。

example: Say, still not resolved.
ja: 解決済み、問題が完全に処理された状態。

example: Very polite. They will appreciate that phrasing.
ja: 礼儀正しい、適切な表現。

example: We call this misplaced baggage.
zh: 行李錯放，行李不見但在錯誤地點。
ja: 誤置された手荷物、紛失ではなく間違った場所にある。

example: Your boarding pass and baggage claim tag.
The small sticker on ticket?
You can claim a essentials allowance for tonight.
zh: 基本必需品津貼，補助購買牙刷、衣物等。
ja: 必需品手当、歯ブラシや衣類などの購入補助。

example: Let's order you a taxi right now.
Ask, how long is the estimated wait time?
My impulse always wins.
Mute means silent.
ja: ミュート、音声を消すこと。

example: You unmute yourself to speak.
ja: ミュート解除、音声を出すこと。

example: Someone said you are breaking up.
Let's circle back.
Organic means grown without chemicals or pesticides.
zh: 有機的，無化學農藥栽培。
ja: オーガニック、化学薬品や農薬を使わずに育てられた。

example: It means the food comes from nearby farms.
These apples look very ripe.
Buying a large amount for a cheaper price.
Working for free to help other people.
ja: ボランティア、無償で他人を助けること。

example: We are sorting donations today.
Donations means things people give for free.
This community is very generous.
ja: 寛大な、喜んで与えること。

example: We are going on a road trip.
ja: ロードトリップ、車での長距離旅行。

example: Lake District is a national park.
A trail is a path you walk through nature.
ja: トレイル、自然の中の小道。

example: A steep trail is difficult and tiring.
We sleep outside in a campsite.
We should check the forecast before leaving.
Road trips teach you to go with the flow.
ja: 流れに身を任せる、あまり心配しないこと。

example: A schedule is a list of times and events.
ja: スケジュール、時間とイベントのリスト。

example: Performing means someone's singing or dancing live.
ja: パフォーマンス、生で歌ったり踊ったりすること。

example: Excuse me, where is the restroom please?
In UK people also say toilet or loo.
ja: イギリス英語のトイレの俗語。

example: This area with food is called the food court.
He is called an artisan, makes things by hand.
ja: 職人、手作りで物を作る人。

example: Rescue means someone saved these dogs.
They were abandoned, left alone with no owner.
ja: 見捨てられた、所有者がいない状態。

example: Temperament describes the personality of an animal.
ja: 気質、動物の性格を表す。

example: Vet means doctor for animals.
Vaccinations means the injection to prevent illness.
Membership means you pay monthly to use this gym.
ja: 会員資格、ジムの利用権。

example: Pause means stop for a while and come back.
Personal trainer means someone who teaches me exercises.
ja: パーソナルトレーナー。

example: I have discomfort in my knee.
Warming up prepares your body before exercising.
ja: ウォームアップ、運動前の準備。

example: Injuries means getting hurt during exercise.
You walk or run on a treadmill.
That one with the weights is called a bench press.
ja: ベンチプレス、ウエイトトレーニングの一種。

example: Sometimes music plays and nothing happens. That means you are on hold.
They will ask for your identification first.
It is for everyday use.
You put money aside there for future use.
Debit means I spend only what I have.
Sustainable means made without harming the environment.
ja: 持続可能な、環境を害さない。

example: Pollution means damage to air and water.
Second hand means used but still good.
It's grown without chemicals, softer and more natural.
ja: オーガニックコットン。

example: Workers were treated fairly and paid properly.
zh: 道德製造，公平對待工人。
ja: 倫理的に作られた、公正な労働条件。

example: Thinking carefully before you buy, not just impulse.
zh: 有意識的購物，慎重消費。
ja: 意識的な買い物、衝動買いしないこと。

example: It means your brother's wife.
Nephew is boy.
Niece is girl.
Elderly sounds more respectful than very old.
ja: 高齢の、より敬意を表す言い方。

example: Extended means beyond just parents' children.
Hiking means walking in nature.
ja: ハイキング、自然の中を歩くこと。

example: Trying out means experimenting with new things.
ja: 試す、新しいことを試みること。

example: Fluent means speaking without stopping or hesitating.
ja: 流暢な、ためらわずに話すこと。

example: The teacher said, past perfect tense.
Commute means traveling to work daily.
Rush hour is the busiest time when everyone travels together.
ja: ラッシュアワー、交通の混雑時間。

example: That is your transit card for paying fares.
Fare means the money for the journey.
This train is very packed.
Get ready to squeeze through.
Urgent means needs attention right now.
In the loop means kept informed.
ja: 情報を共有している状態。

example: Americano is just black coffee.
ja: アメリカーノ、ブラックコーヒー。

example: Orientation is an introduction to your new workplace.
zh: 新員工培訓，介紹工作環境。
ja: オリエンテーション、新しい職場の紹介。

example: Your supervisor is your direct boss.
ja: スーパーバイザー、直属の上司。

example: Probation period is a trial period.
Just say, I apologize.
A tight knit community means everyone knows each other.
ja: 結束の固いコミュニティ。

example: Carefree means no worries at all.
ja: のんきな、心配事がない。

example: Adulting means dealing with adult responsibilities.
ja: 大人としての責任を果たすこと。

example: Fond memory means something you remember lovingly.
ja: 愛情を込めて覚えている思い出。

example: Next door means the flat beside mine.
Block means a section of this building.
The landlord is the person who owns and rents this building.
Residence means people who live here.
Casual interview means less formal interview.
ja: カジュアル面接、あまり堅苦しくない面接。

example: Company culture means how people work together inside the company.
Laid back means very relaxed and informal.
ja: リラックスしていて非公式な。

example: Ambition is a powerful goal you work hard towards.
It feels out of reach.
A clear picture of what you want to build.
ja: ビジョン、目標の明確なイメージ。

example: Self-doubt means not believing in yourself.
ja: 自己疑念、自分を信じられないこと。

example: Push through means continue despite the fear.
ja: 困難を乗り越えて続けること。

example: Welcome to the night market.
A stall is a small outside shop.
When you pay, say, here you go.
This is the natural history museum.
Fossils are old bones of dead animals.
Extinct means the animal doesn't exist anymore.
Sleep deprived means not having enough sleep.
Exhausted means extremely tired.
I need to fix my routine.
I have been job hunting.
I have submitted my resume.
I haven't heard back yet.
ja: まだ返事が来ていない。

example: I am a hard working person.
I am eager to learn.
Employer means the person who gives job.
I look forward to hearing from you.
ja: 返事を楽しみにしている。

example: This is the city center.
We take the subway.
That feeling is called confidence.
Welcome to my home.
I also made fresh lemonade.
I love homemade meals.
Consistency brings good results.
Running is excellent cardio.
Stretching prevents many injuries.
I want to build strength.
I really enjoy deadlifts.
ja: デッドリフト、重量挙げの一種。

example: Today, we will review your assessment.
Your score is 82.
Improve means make something better.
Feedback means advice about your work.
ja: フィードバック、仕事に関する助言。

example: A banquet is a large formal dinner.
Reservation means booking a place early.
Guests are the people you invite.
Menu is the list of food and drinks.
An aisle is a long row between shelves.
A basket is a small container.
Discount means the price is lower than normal.
Receipt shows what I bought.
Carrot shows how pure the gold is.
ja: カラット、金の純度の単位。

example: A gemstone can be a diamond, ruby, or sapphire.
Each coach is one section of the train.
A platform is the area where passengers wait.
You can buy a ticket here at the counter.
Schedule tells you when trains leave and arrive.
Boarding means passengers enter the plane.
Departure is when plane leaves.
An aisle seat is next to the walkway.
There is a weight limit of 23 kilograms.
A fee is an additional charge.
Carry-on means small bag you take on board.
It's the moving belt for luggage.
Customs is where officers check belongings.
Transfer means changing planes during your journey.
Screening means checking passengers and bags.
ja: 検査、セキュリティチェック。

example: It's a small train between terminals.
I'd like to check in.
Return ticket means you're flying back later.
Please place your suitcase on the scale.
Here is your baggage receipt.
Carry-on means small bag you take on board.
A banquet is a large formal dinner.
Discount means the price is lower than normal.
Receipt shows what I bought.
Carrot shows how pure the gold is.
ja: カラット、金の純度の単位。

example: A gemstone can be a diamond, ruby, or sapphire.
Each coach is one section of the train.
A platform is the area where passengers wait.
Boarding means passengers enter the plane.
Departure is when plane leaves.
An aisle seat is next to the walkway.
There is a weight limit of 23 kilograms.
A fee is an additional charge.
Carry-on means small bag you take on board.
